message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Lord Fletcher's (Lord Fletcher Inn) (Rancho Mirage)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 232 reviews (listed as CLOSED); Tripadvisor presence with legacy reviews; legacy Google footprint exists but current count not directly verified [UNVERIFIED exact Google count]
- Sentiment: Legacy: mostly positive, nostalgic — prime rib and Sinatra-era atmosphere consistently praised; some reviewers noted dated decor/food in final years. No current operating sentiment (closed since 2020).
- Longevity: Operated 1966-2020 (54 years) under Fletcher family (founder Ron Fletcher; passed to son Michael Fletcher). Closed during pandemic. Acquired by Life & Times Hospitality (Mike and Matt French) with reopening targeted for 2026.
- Credentials: ["Frank Sinatra documented regular for 30+ years (Coachella Valley Independent, multiple press)","Coachella Valley Independent feature: 'End of an Era' (2020)","Recognized as the original restaurant that launched Rancho Mirage's Restaurant Row (Palm Springs Life, local press)","NBC Palm Springs / Roggin Report coverage (2025)"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for historical/nostalgia queries ('Sinatra restaurants Palm Springs', 'Rancho Mirage landmark restaurant', 'Lord Fletcher's history'). Does NOT surface in current 'best restaurants Rancho Mirage 2026', 'best steakhouse Rancho Mirage', or 'fine dining Rancho Mirage' lists — those are dominated by The Edge Steakhouse (Ritz-Carlton), The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente, Wally's Desert Turtle, Willie's Modern Fare, and Foxy's Kitchen + Bar. Appears in 'English pub Palm Springs' queries but flagged as closed.
- Site structured data: Two parallel sites exist: legacy lordfletcher.com (SSL certificate verification failed on fetch) and new lordfletcherinn.com. The new site has no schema.org markup, no hours, no menu, no phone, no social links — only an email signup, address (70385 Highway 111, Rancho Mirage, CA 92270), and 'Opening 2026' tagline. Equestrian/polo brand imagery suggests a repositioning. Agent-readability is currently very low.
- Visibility gap score: 8
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Lord Fletcher's Inn in Rancho Mirage:

Lord Fletcher Inn is a legendary dining club returning to Rancho Mirage, California, with an opening planned for 2026. Founded by Ron Fletcher, the iconic restaurant originally opened in 1966.

The restaurant closed during the pandemic, and it was originally announced it would not reopen. However, Lord Fletcher's is making a comeback under new ownership at 70385 Highway 111 in Rancho Mirage. It was recently acquired by Life & Times Hospitality, a group that operates The Red Dog Saloon and Pioniertown Motel in Pioneertown, as well as The Copper Room in Yucca Valley, and is run by Mike and Matt French.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best steakhouses in Rancho Mirage for 2026:

## Top Steakhouse Recommendations

**The Edge Steakhouse**
The Edge Steakhouse in Rancho Mirage is renowned for its exquisite steaks and seafood, located within The Ritz-Carlton and combining "6-star dining in a beautiful venue" with breathtaking views. The steaks, particularly the Wagyu, are frequently praised as "the best we've ever had."

**Foxy's Kitchen + Bar**
Foxy's Kitchen + Bar is a family-run spot from longtime Coachella Valley hospitality professionals, where steaks and osso buco are favorites complemented by an array of seafood, pastas, and vegan options. The filet is "outstanding perfectly cooked and packed with rich, robust flavor every time."

**The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente Resort Casino Spa Rancho Mirage**
Recognized by Wine Spectator and other prestigious awards, this restaurant offers prime steaks, fresh seafood, and an extensive wine selection in an elegant setting.

**Kobe Japanese Steakhouse**
Founded in 1978, Kobe Japanese Steakhouse offers a warm and entertaining environment, with highly trained teppan chefs serving the finest ingredients, while authentic Japanese antiques and loyal staff contribute to the restaurant's timeless charm.

These restaurants offer a range of upscale dining experiences with high-quality steaks and excellent service in the Rancho Mirage area.

Links: [{"title":"Becoming a City - Welcome to Rancho Mirage","url":"https://rm50th.com/history/becoming_a_city.php"},{"title":"Lord Fletcher Inn - Fine Dining Restaurant in Rancho Mirage, California","url":"http://www.lordfletcher.com/"},{"title":"English-themed Lord Fletcher’s pioneered Rancho Mirage fine dining","url":"https://acesgolf.com/english-themed-lord-fletchers-pioneered-rancho-mirage-fine-dining/"},{"title":"End of an Era: Remembering the Legendary Lord Fletcher's—a Favorite of Frank Sinatra, and a Classic Prime Rib Destination - Coachella Valley Independent","url":"https://cvindependent.com/2020/09/end-of-an-era-remembering-the-legendary-lord-fletcher-s-a-favorite-of-frank-sinatra-and-a-classic-prime-rib-destination/"},{"title":"Oldest Restaurants in the Desert Bank on Good Food, Service, Ambiance","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/the-oldest-restaurants-in-the-desert/"},{"title":"Lord fletcher's palm desert","url":"https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/66f3d99c8ac8326d8bba4f4b/66fa2a96efc2ea9ee115229b_47945374317.pdf"},{"title":"Lord Fletcher’s: Rancho Mirage Classic Dining Discovery | Peter Moruzzi's Mid-Century","url":"https://petermoruzzi.com/2013/03/10/lord-fletchers-rancho-mirage-classic-dining-discovery/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Lord Fletcher's, Sinatra, Rancho Mirage, and Restaurant Row:

## History and Opening

Ron Fletcher opened Lord Fletcher's in 1966 as an English countryside inn-inspired restaurant with a collection of authentic art, antique swords and lances, tapestries, beer steins, and bric-a-brac, all selected by Fletcher and shipped from England.

## Restaurant Row Connection

The city's 1979 amended general plan specifically mentioned "the creation of 'Restaurant Row,' which should be recognized as an asset to the City." Lord Fletcher's was the original restaurant that launched "Restaurant Row" in Rancho Mirage, California. Restaurant Row began developing in earnest in the 1970s and came into its own in the '80s.

## Frank Sinatra Connection

Frank Sinatra, who became a close friend of Fletcher and his son Michael, was perhaps the restaurant's best known and most loyal customer. Frank was a regular for more than 30 years. He liked the Lord Fletcher's version of the Brandy Alexander so much that he would dine at home or elsewhere and then send a servant to the restaurant for a Brandy Alexander to go.

## Atmosphere and Guests

A portrait of Sir Winston Churchill greets guests and reflects the intimacy and familiarity of the restaurant felt by the guests, who included Rancho Mirage homeowners Lucille Ball, Walter Annenberg, Kirk Douglas, and Gerald Ford. Other regulars included Bob Hope, Elizabeth Taylor, Kirk Douglas, Walter Annenberg, George Hamilton, Steve McQueen and Gerald Ford.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Lord Fletcher's English pub in the Coachella Valley:

## Overview
Lord Fletchers is a rare institution that has been around forever in the Coachella Valley, offering classic English flair and fare, as well as royal flavors. Lord Fletchers is located at 70-385 Hwy 111, Rancho Mirage Ca, 92270.

## History
Opened by Michael's father, Ron Fletcher, in 1966, the restaurant established itself as an intrinsic piece of local history. Since opening over 50 years ago by Ron Fletcher, the restaurant has been passed down to his son Michael, who continues to uphold the Lord Fletchers tradition.

## Atmosphere
Upon entering, the space transports you to an old English Inn, complete with ornate paintings, British trinkets, and dark wood hues. A large fireplace separates the Pub from the Shakespeare dining room, creating a super cozy atmosphere.

## Famous Patrons
The pub is where Frank Sinatra liked to dine, as did Lucille Ball. The Chairman of the Board, or Ol' Blue Eyes, had his regular seat in the bar—his signed photo now hangs over it.

## Signature Dishes
The main attraction, even more than Sinatra, was the prime rib. It was widely acknowledged as the best in the valley. Entrée items include soup of the day or house salad, as well as choices like Pot Roast with red cabbage and apple sauce, Olde Fashion English Chicken and Dumplings, Whole Rack of New Zealand Lamb, and more.

## Recent Status
There are signs of movement at Lord Fletcher's in Rancho Mirage and other longtime restaurant spaces. This appears to reference recent activity regarding the restaurant's future.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Life and Times Hospitality, Lord Fletcher's, and Mike and Matt French:

Life & Times Hospitality is a group known for operating The Red Dog Saloon and Pioneertown Motel in Pioneertown, as well as The Copper Room in Yucca Valley. It's a family business run by Mike and Matt French.

Lord Fletcher's was founded by Ron Fletcher and originally opened in 1966. Over the years it gained a following from high-profile people like Frank Sinatra. The restaurant unfortunately closed during the pandemic. It was recently acquired by Life & Times Hospitality.

Brothers Mike and Matt French are two-thirds of design and development studio Life & Times and first visited Pioneertown well over a decade ago, where they found, fell in love with, and eventually bought its namesake motel. The brothers moved from L.A. to a slower pace of life and a house in Pioneertown in 2014.

content.text: I have enough to verify the claims. The legacy site SSL failure confirmed, the new site has no structured data, the acquisition story checks out, and current best-of lists confirm Lord Fletcher's does not surface for active dining queries.

confirms: Yelp lists Lord Fletcher's Restaurant as CLOSED with 232 reviews at 70-385 Hwy 111 (confirmed via Yelp search result snippet showing '232 Reviews' and 'CLOSED').
Tripadvisor has legacy reviews (multiple Tripadvisor user-review URLs surfaced, e.g., 'British Pub in the Desert' and 'Old, Established, Traditional').
Operated 1966-2020 under the Fletcher family — founder Ron Fletcher, passed to son Michael — confirmed in Coachella Valley Independent, Aces Golf feature, Palm Springs Life.

Closed during pandemic and acquired by Life & Times Hospitality (Mike and Matt French), reopening targeted 2026 — confirmed via whatnow.com, NBC Palm Springs Roggin Report (2026-02-28), and Life & Times team page.
Frank Sinatra was a 30+ year regular — confirmed in Coachella Valley Independent 'End of an Era' (2020) and Coachella Valley Weekly.
Lord Fletcher's launched Rancho Mirage's Restaurant Row — confirmed in Aces Golf feature citing the 1979 amended general plan.
Coachella Valley Independent 'End of an Era' (2020) article exists at cited URL.
NBC Palm Springs / Roggin Report 2026 coverage exists (2026-02-28 update).
Legacy lordfletcher.com SSL certificate verification failed on independent fetch ('unable to verify the first certificate').

New lordfletcherinn.com landing page has no JSON-LD/schema markup, no hours, no menu, no phone, no social links — only email signup, address, and 'Opening 2026' framing with polo/equestrian imagery (Palm Springs Polo Club reference).

Does NOT surface in current 'best steakhouse Rancho Mirage 2026' lists — those are dominated by The Edge Steakhouse (Ritz-Carlton), The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente, Foxy's Kitchen + Bar, and Kobe Japanese Steakhouse. Lord Fletcher's is absent from all current best-of results.
Surfaces strongly for historical/nostalgia queries (Sinatra, Restaurant Row, English pub Coachella Valley) — confirmed via multiple feature-article hits.
flagged_as_unverified: Exact current Google review count was not directly verified (claim itself flagged as unverified — consistent).

Specific characterization of 'mostly positive, nostalgic' sentiment with 'dated decor/food in final years' caveat — directional review snippets ('Old, Established, Traditional', 'British Pub in the Desert') align with this but no quantitative sentiment data was confirmed.

Wally's Desert Turtle and Willie's Modern Fare specifically named as current top competitors — the current best-of results I retrieved did not name Wally's or Willie's; instead Foxy's Kitchen + Bar and Kobe Japanese Steakhouse appeared alongside The Edge and Agua Caliente. The dominance-by-others claim holds but the exact competitor list is partially unverified.

'Appears in English pub Palm Springs queries but flagged as closed' — search surfaced English-pub feature articles but Yelp/Tripadvisor closed-flag behavior in those query results not directly verified.
input.notes: Core claims hold up well. Acquisition by Life & Times Hospitality (Mike/Matt French), 1966-2020 Fletcher family operation, Sinatra heritage, Restaurant Row pioneering role, legacy SSL failure, and new site's lack of structured data are all independently confirmed. Current absence from best-of-Rancho-Mirage lists is verified (Edge Steakhouse / Agua Caliente / Foxy's dominate). Only minor refinement: the exact competitor lineup named in the assessment (Wally's, Willie's) was not the specific set surfaced in my searches, though the broader claim that current best-of lists are dominated by other venues holds. Visibility gap of 8 and anchor-reference convertibility tier are well-supported given closed-Yelp-listing + zero schema on new site + strong historical-only surfacing.
